AMD.reasoning:
CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5 after close — 3 trading days away. This is a live binary catalyst, but the setup going into it is poor: the stock is already extended after a parabolic +76% April run, making it a sell-the-news candidate rather than a buy-the-catalyst setup.

NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Mixed. Positive: DA Davidson upgrade to Buy ($375 PT, April 29), Meta $60B infrastructure deal, TSMC 2nm first-mover advantage, Lisa Su 'year 2 of a 10-year AI build-out' thesis. Negative: Northland simultaneous downgrade to Market Perform ($260 PT) citing stretched valuation, AMD trading at $354 vs. analyst consensus average PT of $296 (already 20% above average target), 25% tariff overhang on MI325X, and Evercore bull target of $358 nearly reached — 'much of the good news is priced in.' The analyst community is literally splitting at the ATH. Rolled-up sentiment: MIXED, with bearish bias from a valuation/positioning standpoint.

INSIDER ACTIVITY: Net selling. CEO Lisa Su sold ~$16M in shares in March 2026. Board-level distribution is 188 sells vs. 83 buys (2:1 ratio). No insider buying signal in the last 30 days. This is a 'don't chase' posture signal.

TECHNICAL SETUP — POOR/EXHAUSTED GOING IN: Daily RSI 79.4–86.5 across all timeframes (severely overbought). Price is 52.6% above 50 SMA and 70.5% above 200 EMA — extreme historical stretch. Z-score vs. 50-day: 2.6 standard deviations. Bollinger pct_b: 0.93 (hugging upper band). ROC 21d: +74.26% — the parabolic move is already complete. Hourly ADX: 15.89 (weak intraday trend strength), hourly relative volume 0.48x — no informed accumulation occurring in the hours before earnings. MACD histogram is positive on daily but no fresh bullish crossover. The chart is NOT building a constructive base or coiling; it is in the exhaustion phase of a vertical move.

LESSONS APPLIED: The most recent trade lesson (2026-04-30, NVDA) directly parallels this setup: RSI 81–87 + extended price + stop triggered at hourly Fib 61.8% with no bullish confirmation candle. Confidence reduced 5–10 points for this pattern match. Per system prompt: 'A catalyst exists but the technical setup going in is poor (already extended)' — lean toward neutral or bearish. The catalyst is real but pricing a beat-and-raise into a +76% month is high risk.

TRADE THESIS: Fade into earnings. Entry on any bounce to resistance zone near $355–358 (ATH + Evercore bull target zone). Target: ~$316 (hourly Fib 23.6% retracement support / prior consolidation base). Stop: $370 (above ATH with buffer — if price breaks to new ATH on volume before earnings, thesis is invalidated). Expected catalyst magnitude on a beat: 5–8% up; on a miss or beat-but-lower-guide: 10–20% down. Risk/reward favors the short side given the already-priced-in bull case.
